articles.title: Numerator January Consumer Price Index Reveals Prices for Everyday Goods Up 1.8% vs. Year Ago
articles.summary: January 2026 Marks First Month-over-Month Decline in Inflation Since February 2025CHICAGO, Feb. 11, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Numerator, a consumer data and technology company, released its January 2026 Numerator Consumer Price Index (CPI) with an advance read on inflation trends across everyday consumer goods.

articles.title: Consumer Price Index: Inflation Holds Steady At 2.7% In July, Lower Than Expected
articles.summary: Inflation held steady in July as the headline figure for the Consumer Price Index was at 2.7% year-over-year, unchanged from June and lower than the expected 2.8% growth. The index for shelter rose 0.2 percent in July and was the primary factor in the all ...

articles.title: Consumer Price Index: Inflation Cools To 2.3% In April, Lower Than Expected
articles.summary: According to the Bureau of Labor Statistics, the headline figure for the Consumer Price Index was at 2.3% year-over-year, down from 2.4% in March and lower than the expected 2.4% growth. On a monthly basis, headline and core prices rose 0.2%, lower than ...

articles.title: Consumer prices rose in August. See which of your grocery items went up
articles.summary: Consumer prices rose 0.4% last month after increasing 0.2% in July, the largest increase in seven months. The Consumer Price Index, released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics, is a common measure of inflation, as it shows the change over time in the prices ...
